mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.02 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, causing equity valuations to decline over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold, breaking the recession fear channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in long-term borrowing costs and declines in housing starts indicate tightening credit conditions in the real estate sector, which transmits to broader corporate balance sheets and suppresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS rises and HOUST falls, with a directional hit rate below 40% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which translates into immediate positive price adjustments in equity markets over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to generate positive 5-day forward returns during recovery phases, with directional accuracy dropping below 50% in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP demonstrates sustained aggregate demand expansion, which supports corporate revenue trajectories and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day equity returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across all macro cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month inflation increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above the 0.4% threshold are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases, invalidating the tightening fear transmission.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, which boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 60-day returns in over 60% of triggered observations, breaking the earnings transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year housing starts decline exceeding 10 percent signals a sharp contraction in residential construction activity, which reduces related sector revenues and drags down broader equity market returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST drops beyond the 10% threshold are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, negating the housing drag effect.
